An accessory is a person who assists in the commission of a crime, but who does not actually participate in the commission of the crime as a joint principal. The distinction between an accessory and a principal is a question of fact and degree:

The principal is the one whose acts or omissions, accompanied by the relevant mens rea (Latin for "guilty mind"), are the most immediate cause of the actus reus (Latin for "guilty act").

If two or more people are directly responsible for the actus reus, they can be charged as joint principals (see common purpose). The test to distinguish a joint principal from an accessory is whether the defendant independently contributed to causing the actus reus rather than merely giving generalised and/or limited help and encouragement.

Source: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Accessory_(legal_term)

一名自稱留學澳洲的香港學生，昨日將數段聲稱是友人在當地入屋爆竊、惡搞房東及刑毀車輛的短片上載到個人facebook，迅即惹起公憤。網民批評他知情不報，還上載分享，沾沾自喜，等同共犯，於是將港生起底，並聲言已向當地警方舉報。其後該學生發出聲明，強調自己只是「睇同拍片」並無落手破壞。惟執業大律師指，只要有拍片及片中有對話，證明有參與，即構成協同犯法，實為犯法行為，留學生不應助紂為虐。當地警方發言人稱，如有足夠資料，會嚴正執法。

上載短片的學生，在其個人facebook中自稱在澳洲昆士蘭大學讀書，居於當地布里斯班。昨日他將數段在當地的「生活點滴」短片上載到fb，當中包括友人入屋爆竊、肆意以噴漆在多輛車身上噴上中文「粗口」字、退租前以噴漆將單位浴室塗鴉、將白砂糖混入洗衣液，與及啤酒溝菜油倒入洗衣機等等。片中惡搞房東的短片錄得，拍片者與犯案者均說流利廣東話，拍片者在旁笑個不停，沒有阻止之餘，還稱是「啱」、「俾個Like你」。

網民看過短片後，除了指摘犯事者外，更對拍片者的行為表示不滿，認為他知情不報，反而拍片分享，等同共犯，有人聲言已向當地警方報案。《蘋果》今早向昆士蘭警方查詢，警長Raymond指知情者可致電(07)131 444與警方聯絡，亦可經當地警方網站作出舉報，如證據充足，警方會作出跟進及調查。Raymond透露，當地的罪案主要為入屋爆竊及盜竊等，塗鴉車輛的個案則較少，但強調有關行為均有機會觸犯法例，有可能會被法律制裁。

執業大律師陸偉雄亦指，雖然拍片者聲稱只是拍片，未有參與任何犯法行為，但如他在犯案現場出現，既拍片又與犯事者對話，均可構成「協同犯法」。加上有關行為實屬不當，「俾外國人睇到都唔好，會對本地留學生有壞印象，喺文明的社會係唔應該發生。」他認為學生不應貪玩，切勿幹出犯法行為。

(1) Any person who wilfully and unlawfully destroys or damages any property is guilty of an offence which, unless otherwise stated, is a misdemeanour, and the person is liable, if no other punishment is provided, to imprisonment for 5 years.

(2) For this section, other than punishment in special cases, clause 11, the destruction or damage of property that is a thing mentioned in section 566(11) is presumed to be done without the owner's consent until the contrary is proved.

(3) For this section, punishment in special cases, clause 11, the destruction or damage of property is, until the contrary is proved, presumed to be done—

(a) without the owner's consent; and

(b) if the property is fixed in a cemetery or at a crematorium—

(i) without the lawful consent of the entity (if any) responsible for managing and administering the cemetery or crematorium; and

(ii) not in the reasonable belief that lawful consent mentioned in subparagraph (i) has been given.
